answer:
Let \( x_{i} = \frac{\pi}{2} + \frac{i}{2n}\pi \), \( i = 1, 2, \dots, n \).

Then, \( \sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin ^{2} x_{i} = \frac{n}{2} \).

Since \( \sin 2x = 2\sin x\cos x \), we have \( \left|\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin 2x_{i}\right| = \left|\sum_{i=1}^{n} 2\sin x_{i}\cos x_{i}\right| \).

By Cauchy-Schwarz inequality, \( \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_{i}b_{i}\right)^{2} \leq \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} a_{i}^{2}\right)\left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} b_{i}^{2}\right) \), where \( a_{i} = \sin x_{i} \) and \( b_{i} = \cos x_{i} \), we have

\( \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin x_{i}\cos x_{i}\right)^{2} \leq \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin ^{2} x_{i}\right)\left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} \cos ^{2} x_{i}\right) \).

Since \( \sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin ^{2} x_{i} = \frac{n}{2} \), \( \sum_{i=1}^{n} \cos ^{2} x_{i} = \frac{n}{2} \), we have

\( \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin x_{i}\cos x_{i}\right)^{2} \leq \frac{n^{2}}{4} \).

Thus, \( \left|\sum_{i=1}^{n} \sin 2x_{i}\right| = \left|\sum_{i=1}^{n} 2\sin x_{i}\cos x_{i}\right| \leq 2\sqrt{\frac{n^{2}}{4}} = \boxed{\sqrt{n}} \).
